source_depth_status: source-backed draft; not yet mature under the 100-page target

sources.title: Watershed infarct - MedGen - NCBI
sources.notes: Used for aliases, SNOMED CT mapping, and source discovery metadata. MedGen identifies watershed, arterial border-zone, boundary-zone, and vascular border-zone infarct terminology.

sources.title: Diagnosis and treatment of Watershed strokes: a narrative review
sources.notes: Used for watershed-specific imaging patterns, cortical versus internal border-zone framing, string-of-pearls pattern, and differential diagnosis with lacunar infarcts, embolic infarcts, small vessel disease, and demyelination.

sources.title: Border Zone Infarcts: Pathophysiologic and Imaging Characteristics
sources.notes: Used for external versus internal border-zone imaging language, wedge/ovoid cortical patterns, rosary-like internal patterns, and imaging-management context. ResearchGate copy was used only as discovery evidence; DOI is the authoritative source URL.

sources.title: An MRI Based Ischemic Stroke Classification - A Mechanism Oriented Approach
sources.notes: Used to connect MRI stroke pattern classification with mechanism-oriented cohort labels and subtype distinctions.

sources.title: Etiopathology, Clinical and Imaging Characteristics of Border Zone Strokes
sources.notes: Supplemental source for border-zone imaging characteristics; avoid overgeneralizing prevalence or site-specific cohort findings.

sources.title: Border-zone and watershed infarctions
sources.notes: Used as supporting source for border-zone/watershed terminology and mechanism uncertainty; abstract source only in this environment.

sources.title: ACR Appropriateness Criteria: Cerebrovascular Diseases-Stroke and Stroke-Related Conditions
sources.notes: Used for stroke imaging pathway and modality context. ScienceDirect page provided abstract and method/source metadata; Weill Cornell page provides bibliographic metadata.

sources.title: ACR Appropriateness Criteria Cerebrovascular Diseases-Stroke and Stroke-Related Conditions - Weill Cornell bibliographic page
sources.notes: Used to confirm guideline publication metadata, DOI, and PubMed ID when the full ACR page is abstract-limited.

sources.title: A narrative review of the pathophysiology of ischemic stroke in carotid plaques: hemodynamic and embolic mechanisms
sources.notes: Supports mixed hypoperfusion and embolic mechanisms and includes watershed schematic/DWI examples.

sources.title: The Vulnerability of Vessels Involved in the Role of Embolism and Hypoperfusion in Ischemic Cerebrovascular Diseases
sources.notes: Supports vessel vulnerability, microembolism, collateral flow, and border-zone pattern interpretation.

sources.title: Cerebral border zone infarction: an etiological study
sources.notes: Supports internal/cortical/mixed border-zone subtypes, stenosis, and imaging examples.

sources.title: Cerebral border zone infarction: an etiological study - PubMed record
sources.notes: Provides DOI, PMID, PMCID, and figure list for the open-access etiological study.

sources.title: Pearls & Oy-sters: Iatrogenic relative hypotension leading to diffuse internal borderzone infarctions and coma
sources.notes: Case-level evidence of diffuse internal borderzone DWI lesions after relative hypotension.

sources.title: Radiologic-pathologic autopsy correlation of an internal watershed infarct, a case report
sources.notes: Correlates internal watershed MRI with neuropathology; case-level evidence only.

sources.title: Collateral ability of the circle of Willis in unilateral internal carotid artery occlusion: border zone infarcts and clinical symptoms
sources.notes: Supports collateral-flow context in ICA occlusion and border-zone infarcts.

sources.title: Prognostic value of preoperative border-zone infarcts on early postoperative outcomes of carotid endarterectomy after acute ischemic stroke
sources.notes: Supports revascularization-context outcome caveats for carotid stenosis cases.

sources.title: Paracentral strip infarcts of the middle cerebral artery: borderzone ischaemia or cortical artery occlusion?
sources.notes: Supports the caveat that strip-like cortical infarcts can reflect cortical artery occlusion, not only border-zone ischemia.

sources.title: Cerebral bloodflow and oxygen metabolism in borderzone and territorial infarcts due to symptomatic carotid artery occlusion
sources.notes: Supports hemodynamic mechanism and oxygen-metabolism context in border-zone infarcts.

sources.title: Clinical and imaging features in different inner border-zone infarct patterns
sources.notes: Supports pattern-level differences among inner border-zone infarcts.

sources.title: Clinical Significance of FLAIR Vascular Hyperintensities in Borderzone Infarcts
sources.notes: Supports FVH as a hemodynamic/prognostic clue in border-zone infarcts.

sources.title: Association between impaired carbon dioxide reactivity and ischemic lesions in arterial border zone territories in unilateral ICA occlusion
sources.notes: Supports impaired vasomotor reactivity as a hemodynamic covariate for internal border-zone lesions.

sources.title: Hemodynamic study of internal carotid artery stenosis and occlusion: value of combined isotopic measurements of regional cerebral blood flow and blood volume
sources.notes: Older source supporting cerebral hemodynamic reserve concepts; use as historical/narrow evidence.

sources.title: Watershed cerebral infarction - Radiopaedia
sources.notes: Teaching/reference source with concise imaging terminology; use with stronger sources for broad claims.

sources.title: The Radiology Assistant: Vascular territories of the Brain
sources.notes: Useful for territory-based localization and visual teaching around ACA/MCA/PCA distributions.

sources.title: Stroke Imaging - Learn Neuroradiology
sources.notes: Teaching hub with case and vascular-territory video material useful for humans and agents.

sources.title: Stroke and Its Mimics: Diagnosis and Treatment - Diseases of the Brain, Head and Neck, Spine 2024-2027
sources.notes: Use for mimic-aware review, including seizure, demyelination, tumor, and nonstroke mimics.

sources.title: Ischemic Stroke - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf
sources.notes: Use for broad ischemic stroke framing and subtype hierarchy.

sources.title: 2026 Guideline for the Early Management of Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke: A Guideline From the AHA/ASA
sources.notes: Current guideline context; use treatment statements only at research-context level.

sources.title: Stroke and transient ischaemic attack in over 16s: diagnosis and initial management
sources.notes: Jurisdiction-specific guideline context for stroke/TIA initial management.

sources.title: Emergency Department Evaluation and Management of Patients with TIA and Acute Stroke - Canadian Stroke Best Practices
sources.notes: Useful for CT/MRI and vascular imaging workflow prompts.

sources.title: Acute Ischemic Stroke Treatment - Canadian Stroke Best Practices
sources.notes: Use for research-context treatment and imaging selection language.

sources.title: Imaging of Ischemic Stroke
sources.notes: Broad review with figures relevant to DWI, vascular signs, and perfusion mismatch.

sources.title: Imaging of Ischemic Stroke
sources.notes: General ischemic stroke imaging review useful for watershed context.

sources.title: Neuroimaging Modalities Used for Ischemic Stroke Diagnosis and Monitoring
sources.notes: Supports multi-modality workflow including CT, MRI, vascular, and perfusion imaging.

sources.title: Imaging in Acute Stroke
sources.notes: General acute stroke imaging support.

sources.title: Comparison of CT and MR imaging in ischemic stroke
sources.notes: Supports modality tradeoffs for acute and subacute ischemic infarcts.

sources.title: What's new in imaging of acute stroke?
sources.notes: Useful for modern acute stroke imaging context.

sources.title: Imaging of acute stroke prior to treatment: current practice and evolving techniques
sources.notes: Supports acute imaging selection and early CT signs.

sources.title: Magnetic resonance diffusion-perfusion mismatch in acute ischemic stroke: An update
sources.notes: Useful for perfusion/DWI mismatch language when hemodynamic compromise is in scope.

sources.title: Wake-Up Stroke: Clinical Characteristics, Imaging Findings, and Treatment Option - an Update
sources.notes: General acute stroke imaging support; not watershed-specific.

sources.title: Wake-Up Stroke and Stroke of Unknown Onset: A Critical Review
sources.notes: Supports caveats for lesion timing and imaging-selection claims.

sources.title: Mimics and chameleons in stroke
sources.notes: Supports mimic-aware differential diagnosis beyond imaging pattern alone.

sources.title: Overview of imaging modalities for an Acute Stroke - Stroke Manual
sources.notes: Practical educational source for imaging modality selection.

sources.title: MR-DWI in the acute stroke diagnosis - Stroke Manual
sources.notes: Practical DWI/ADC terminology support.

sources.title: Digital 3D Brain MRI Arterial Territories Atlas
sources.notes: Supports arterial-territory localization and agentic lesion labeling.

sources.title: Mapping the Supratentorial Cerebral Arterial Territories Using 1160 Large Artery Infarcts
sources.notes: Supports mapping ACA/MCA/PCA territories and border-zone localization.

sources.title: Border Zone Infarcts: A Pictorial Review
sources.notes: Pictorial review; use as teaching/figure candidate, not primary authority.

sources.title: Watershed Stroke/Infarction - Physiopedia
sources.notes: Secondary source useful for rehabilitation/context and reference chasing; not used for primary clinical claims.

sources.title: Internal borderzone infarction: clinical and neuroradiological features
sources.notes: Supports internal border-zone clinical and imaging pattern context.

sources.title: Hemodynamic risk zones and stroke from hypoperfusion and embolism
sources.notes: Supports mixed hemodynamic and embolic mechanism framing for border-zone lesions.
